creator: mandree@FreeBSD.org
text:
So, acknowledging that depending on ISP configuration, there may be download issues -- however, I am not at all familiar with the innards of the Go (or Rust or NPM) dependency fetching for that matter:

1. do we have infrastructure in place to support mirroring of Go dependencies?
2. could we leverage this to cache required packages in the FreeBSD network so as to keep ports fetchable? 

I don't recall ever having had lasting IP address family (IPv4 vs. IPv6) based download issues from *.freebsd.org sites, and I would expect Google and similar sites to be rather aggressive with network filtering in what they see as their endeavour to keep the bad guys out (and sometimes overshooting the target).
